scoring.cap_note: Forensic cap applied: a high-severity earnings-quality flag caps the overall score at 55 regardless of category scores (see Red Flags).

rules.rationale: Margin level proxies pricing power and business model quality.
Stable margins indicate durable economics rather than one-off swings.
Scale correlates with resilience and institutional interest.
Leverage entering listing is the primary balance-sheet risk.
Short-term liquidity buffer.
Ability to service debt from operating profit.

rules.evidence: REVENUE CAGR 99.8% over the restated period
rules.rationale: Multi-year compounding, not just the latest pre-IPO year.

rules.evidence: PAT CAGR 300.0% over the restated period
rules.rationale: Multi-year compounding, not just the latest pre-IPO year.

rules.thresholds: every yoy step positive → full points
rules.rationale: Consistency separates trend from a single dressed-up year.

rules.evidence: Issuer P/E at the price band could not be extracted from the Basis for Offer Price chapter.
rules.rationale: Peer-relative pricing from the RHP's own mandated comparison table.
Sanity check independent of the issuer-chosen peer set.

rules.evidence: Needs both issuer P/E and positive PAT CAGR

rules.rationale: Skin in the game after listing.

rules.evidence: OFS ₹0 cr of ₹3,600 cr total (0%) — fresh capital ₹3,600 cr goes to the company, OFS to selling holders
rules.rationale: Exit-heavy offers raise nothing for growth; every manual analyst review leads with this.

rules.evidence: Our Promoters have agreed not to sell, transfer, charge, pledge or otherwise encumber in
rules.rationale: Pledged promoter stakes transmit personal leverage into the stock.
Criminal matters against company/promoters/directors.

rules.evidence: No qualification language detected in the restated auditor's report
rules.rationale: Auditor reservations are the highest-signal governance flag in the document.

rules.evidence: No dividends declared in reported years
rules.rationale: A payout history modestly evidences real distributable cash.
Each finding is itemised in the Risks tab with its own evidence.

rules.evidence: Issuer RoE 31.3% vs peer median RoNW 33.9%
rules.rationale: Return spread vs the listed peers named in the RHP.

rules.evidence: Company claims: “…the largest…” — note this is the issuer's own characterisation
rules.rationale: Claims are recorded but explicitly labelled as company-sourced.
Returns on capital are the core of business quality compounding.
Returns on capital are the core of business quality compounding.

rules.evidence: Needs both CFO and positive PAT
rules.rationale: Profit that never becomes cash is the classic earnings-quality failure.

objects.purpose: for general corporate purposes shall not exceed 25% of the Gross Proceeds.
Further, capital expenditure towards the stated Objects may also be accelerated, due to early completion of various
drainage. As per quotes taken from Desai Construction Private Limited dated November 24, 2023, Meera
Acquisition of land (by way of lease)

reasoning: Issuer P/E at the price band could not be extracted from the Basis for Offer Price chapter.

flags.detail: Receivables/sales ratio grew 1.84x year-over-year (Beneish DSRI-style signal; >1.4 is a classic manipulation marker).
Sales grew 1.69x in the latest year; Beneish notes extreme growth firms face pressure that predicts manipulation. Verify sustainability.
PAT grew 155% vs revenue 69% in the latest pre-IPO year — margin expansion this sharp just before listing warrants scrutiny (dressing-up risk).

verdict: Mixed fundamentals · Valuation indeterminate · low risk profile

meta.disclaimer: This is an automated document analysis for research and education. It is not investment advice, not a recommendation, and not a SEBI-registered research report. Scores reflect only information inside the uploaded prospectus and stated assumptions.
